CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF OSMAN, DANIEL JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 7.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-COMPLAINANT HOWARD MILLER'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING SEVERANCE OF CROSS-COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE REQUEST TO RECONSIDER IS GRANTED AND THE MOTION IS DENIED. LOGICALLY, THIS CASE SHOULD BE TRIED IN ONE LOCATION. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF PROPERLY BROUGHT THE CASE IN SAN FRANCISCO WHERE DEFENDANT RESIDES AND DID NOT SUE THE STATE; STANDING ALONE DEFENDANT HAS NO BASIS FOR CHANGING VENUE TO MARIN. BY NAMING THE STATE AND BY RELYING UPON THE STATE'S RIGHT TO HAVE THE CASE HEARD IN MARIN, DEFENDANT SEEKS TO CHANGE VENUE. DEFENDANT POSITS ARE NUMBER OF EVENTS THAT MAY HAPPEN; AT THE SAME TIME, THEY MAY NOT. ABSENT AN INDEPENDENT BASIS TO CHANGE VENUE, DEFENDANT CANNOT ACCOMPLISH THE TASK BY NAMING THE STATE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF PROPERLY BROUGHT THE CASE IN SAN FRANCISCO WHERE DEFENDANT RESIDES AND DID NOT SUE THE STATE; STANDING ALONE DEFENDANT HAS NO BASIS FOR CHANGING VENUE TO MARIN. BY NAMING THE STATE AND BY RELYING UPON THE STATE'S RIGHT TO HAVE THE CASE HEARD IN MARIN, DEFENDANT SEEKS TO CHANGE VENUE. DEFENDANT POSITS ARE NUMBER OF EVENTS THAT MAY HAPPEN; AT THE SAME TIME, THEY MAY NOT. ABSENT AN INDEPENDENT BASIS TO CHANGE VENUE, DEFENDANT CANNOT ACCOMPLISH THE TASK BY NAMING THE STATE. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
